channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US airstrikes targeting a Yemeni oil port killed 38 people, Houthis say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. airstrikes targeting the Ras Isa oil port held by Yemen’s Houthi rebels killed 38 people and wounded 102 others, the group said early Friday. The strikes, confirmed by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US lawmakers' bipartisan Taiwan visit signals support despite harsh words and tariffs from Trump
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Republican and Democratic lawmakers made their first trip to Taiwan under the new Trump administration a bipartisan one, aiming to show both Taiwan and China that U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, ELLEN KNICKMEYER and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Law firms, universities and now civil society groups are in Trump's sights for punitive action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — First the nation’s top law firms. Then its premier universities. Now, President Donald Trump is leaning on the advocacy groups that underpin U.S. civil society.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FSU lecturer finds broken doors in building after 2-hour shooting lockdown
item.description: Busted doors and debris were what a lecturer at Florida State University found after being locked down for hours during Thursday's mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: 2 people killed and at least 6 wounded in Florida State shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A gunman opened fire Thursday at Florida State University, killing two people and wounding at least six others, police said. The 20-year-old gunman is the son of a sheriff’s deputy whose former service weapon was used in the shooting, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers move to mandate the display of Ten Commandments and ban pride flags in Alabama schools
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama lawmakers approved a slew of bills on Thursday that would expand the use of Christian texts in public schools and limit protections for LGBTQ+ students, in lockstep with a national conservative agenda that has divided statehouses across the country.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Pizookie coming to BJ’s Restaurants
item.description: The Broccoli Cheddar Pizookie, slated to debut at BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse nationwide, won’t be coming to restaurants after all. Instead, BJ’s will be releasing a Snickers Pizookie. “We've come to our senses.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KRON4

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court keeps hold on Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but sets May arguments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday kept on hold President Donald Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but agreed to hear arguments on the issue in May. Trump’s executive order to end birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House proposes eliminating Head Start funding as part of sweeping budget cuts
item.description: The Trump administration is asking Congress to eliminate funding for Head Start, a move that would cut early education for more than half a million of the nation’s neediest children and child care for their families.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Too cute! Surprise piglet born at Nashville Zoo
item.description: “He responded well to treatment and is now healthy and being hand-raised by our animal care team,” the zoo said.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Brother of man nearly decapitated at Arizona bus stop by hatchet wielding suspect speaks out
item.description: Jacob Couch's family said he is on life support after a hatchet-wielding suspect nearly decapitated him at a Tucson, Arizona, bus stop in an unprovoked attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump officials' defiance over Abrego Garcia's deportation is 'shocking,' appeals court says
item.description: A federal appeals court says it is “shocking” that The Trump administration claims it can’t do anything to free Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison and return him to the U.S. A three-judge panel from the 4th U.S.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Actor Haley Joel Osment booked for 'public intoxication' at California ski resort
item.description: Actor Haley Joel Osment, famous for his role as the child who saw “dead people” in the 1999 thriller “The Sixth Sense” starring Bruce Willis, was reportedly arrested at a ski resort in Mammoth Lakes for public intoxication, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: ICE defends agents who smashed car window during Massachusetts arrest
item.description: Juan Francisco Mendez, 29, is being held at a detention facility in New Hampshire following his arrest by ICE Boston on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, NewsNation

item.title: UFO footage captured by US Navy shows mysterious aircraft launching from sea, expert says
item.description: Footage reportedly captured by a Navy warship shows four UFOs emerging from the ocean before flying off in unison off the coast of California last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration plans to eliminate habitat protections for endangered species
item.description: (AP) -- The Trump administration plans to eliminate habitat protections for endangered and threatened species in a move environmentalists say would lead to the extinction of critically endangered species because of logging, mining, development and other activities.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: St. Pete woman reunites with nurse who saved her life after crash
item.description: A Pinellas County woman got the chance to reunite with the nurse who helped save her life after a crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ announces civil suit against Maine over transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Tuesday announced a civil lawsuit against Maine’s Department of Education for violating Title IX, the federal law against sex discrimination that the Trump administration has said prohibits transgender students from participating in girls’ and women’s sports in schools.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli airstrike hits hospital entrance in Gaza, killing medic and wounding 9 other people
item.description: An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of a field hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people, a hospital spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harvard stands to lose $2.2 billion in federal funding. Researchers fear science will suffer
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, Mass. (AP) — In a high-stakes standoff, President Donald Trump's administration says it will freeze $2.2 billion in federal research grants for Harvard University, which is pushing back on demands for changes to campus policy.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man charged with arson at Pennsylvania governor's home struggled with mental health, brother says
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion over the weekend had struggled with mental illness, twice being treated at a psychiatric hospital, as his life unraveled dramatically in the past few years, his brother said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FL representative says he'll go to El Salvador if wrongly deported man isn't returned this week
item.description: U.S. House Representative Maxwell Frost (D-Fla.) says he'll join other Democrat lawmakers planning to go to El Salvador if a man mistakenly deported isn't returned this week.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has dug in on its contention that his government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A look at the universities with federal funding targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: Harvard University is the latest in a growing list of higher education institutions that had its federal funding targeted by the government in order to comply with the Trump administration's political agenda. The series of threats — and subsequent pauses in funding — to some of the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Counterfeit Ozempic found in U.S. drug supply, FDA warns
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is warning Americans that counterfeit units of Ozempic have recently found their way into the U.S. drug supply.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
